questions.question: Who is Lushkoff?
questions.correct_answer: A beggar who pretends to be a teacher or student to gain sympathy
questions.explanation: Lushkoff lies about his identity because he believes people will not help him if he tells the truth.

questions.question: Who is Sergei in the story?
questions.correct_answer: A lawyer who tries to help Lushkoff change his life
questions.explanation: Sergei advises Lushkoff to stop lying and encourages him to work honestly.

questions.question: Why does Lushkoff lie about his profession?
questions.correct_answer: Because he thinks people will not give him money if he tells the truth
questions.explanation: Lushkoff admits that begging with lies is the only way he can survive.

questions.question: What work does Sergei offer Lushkoff?
questions.explanation: Sergei believes that honest work can help Lushkoff change his life.

questions.question: Who actually chopped the wood for Sergei?
questions.explanation: Olga secretly chopped the wood herself out of pity for Lushkoff.
questions.source_section: Lushkoff’s revelation later in the story

questions.question: How did Olga influence Lushkoff?
questions.correct_answer: Through her kindness, sympathy, and emotional scolding
questions.explanation: Olga’s tears and compassionate behaviour made Lushkoff feel ashamed and inspired him to reform.

questions.question: What job does Lushkoff have after two years?
questions.explanation: Lushkoff reforms his life and earns a respectable living as a notary.

questions.question: Why does Sergei believe he helped Lushkoff change?
questions.correct_answer: Because he gave him advice and forced him to work
questions.explanation: Sergei thinks his advice encouraged Lushkoff to reform.

questions.question: Who does Lushkoff say truly helped him reform?
questions.explanation: Lushkoff explains that Olga’s kindness and tears changed his heart.

questions.question: What is the main message of the story 'The Beggar'?
questions.correct_answer: Kindness and compassion can transform a person's life
questions.explanation: The story shows that sympathy and moral guidance can inspire people to change for the better.

notes.title: Value of Honest Work
notes.content: The story highlights that honest labour helps people regain dignity and self-respect.

notes.title: Power of Compassion
notes.content: Olga’s kindness and emotional support play the biggest role in Lushkoff’s transformation.

facts.fact: Lushkoff used to sing in a Russian choir before becoming a beggar.
Lushkoff eventually becomes a notary earning thirty-five roubles a month.
